title: Model-Prediction Gaps
concept: When measured results exceed what a model predicts from known variables, the gap signals an interaction the model missed—anomalies are where incomplete theories show their edges.

questions.question: Why does a model that correctly predicts most materials sometimes fail for a new one?
options: The model was built from measurements of different materials
The new material has interactions between components the model doesn't account for
The measurements of the new material were performed incorrectly
The model is outdated and needs to be replaced entirely
questions.explanation: Models sum known effects of individual variables. When a new combination produces behavior the sum doesn't predict, it means those variables interact in a way the model doesn't capture—the model isn't wrong about what it includes, it's incomplete about what it excludes.

questions.question: What makes an anomaly—a result higher than the model predicts—valuable rather than just confusing?
options: It proves the original model was completely wrong
It shows exactly where the model's simplifications break down
It means the measurements are more accurate than previous ones
It suggests the model needs better computational power
questions.explanation: An anomaly marks the boundary of what the model captures. The gap between prediction and measurement tells you which interactions you simplified away—it's a map showing where to look next, not a sign the whole framework is broken.

questions.question: Why do models built from individual component effects sometimes miss combined effects?
options: Component effects are measured separately but combine nonlinearly
The individual measurements were performed at different temperatures
Models can only handle three variables at a time
Combined effects require more expensive testing equipment
questions.explanation: Adding two effects together assumes they don't change each other. But when components interact—one component changing how another behaves—the total effect isn't just A plus B. The model built on separate measurements misses what happens when A and B are present together.

questions.question: A research team designs an alloy based on a model, then measures performance that exceeds the prediction. What should they do first?
options: Publish the result and let other teams figure out why
Test which combinations of variables produce the gap
Assume the measurement was wrong and repeat it
Build a completely new model from scratch
questions.explanation: The gap is the signal—it tells you the model is missing something, but not what. Systematically varying the components that might interact shows which combinations produce the unexpected effect, narrowing down where the missing piece lives in the model.
